channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: LeBron James' children: Bronny, Bryce, and Zhuri's paths in sports and fashion
item.description: LeBron James, the NBA legend, has three children with his wife Savannah: Bronny, Bryce, and Zhuri. Bronny, the eldest, is a basketball player who was drafted by the Los Angeles Lakers in 2024. He face...

item.title: Municipal funding cuts and systemic failures exacerbate local economic despair
item.description: The article critiques South Africa's failure to address illegal migration and municipal underfunding, highlighting how constitutional obligations are unmet. It argues that withholding R13.5bn from 69 ...

item.title: Gov't Evacuates 315 Nigerians from South Africa via Final Flight
item.description: The Federal Government of Nigeria has confirmed that the last evacuation flight carrying 315 Nigerian citizens from South Africa is set to land in Lagos on Wednesday, July 15th, 2026. This comes after...

item.title: Rare 19th-Century Street Scenes Reveal Daily Life in Alkmaar, Netherlands
item.description: These rare photographs from the 19th century offer a captivating glimpse into the daily life of Alkmaar, a historic town in the northern Netherlands. Captured during the early days of photography, the...

item.title: Yanga Delays Mwamnyeto Contract Renewal Amid Financial Discussions
item.description: Young Africans Sports Club in Dar es Salaam is delaying decisions on renewing contracts for key players, including captain Bakari Mwamnyeto, due to financial considerations and administrative transiti...

item.title: Zuleika Lopez testifies in Senate impeachment trial of Sara Duterte
item.description: Zuleika Lopez, chief of staff of Vice President Sara Duterte, arrived at the Senate on Tuesday for the fifth day of the impeachment trial against the country's second highest official. She was present...

item.title: Karachi Rangers camp attack mastermind and accomplices arrested
item.description: The mastermind behind the June 27 attack on a Pakistan Rangers camp in Karachi, along with smugglers who supplied weapons to the terrorists, has been arrested, according to Counter Terrorism Departmen...

item.title: Meru National Polytechnic Offers Diverse Courses and Clear Fee Structures
item.description: The article provides a comprehensive overview of Meru National Polytechnic's academic offerings, administrative processes, and recent developments. It highlights the institution's range of courses acr...

item.title: 60-Year-Old Man Balances PhD and Teaching Career with Part-Time Taxi Job
item.description: The article highlights the story of a 60-year-old man who continues to drive a taxi after his classes, despite holding a doctorate and having years of teaching experience. He explains that his decisio...

item.title: Conspiracy Theories Surround Argentina's World Cup Semi-Final Against England
item.description: Messi's Argentina is in the World Cup semi-final against England, but online conspiracy theories claim FIFA is favoring them. Social media is flooded with AI-generated videos showing FIFA President Gi...

item.title: Calgary Fire Department Rescues Child from Storm Pond, but Tragic Outcome Follows
item.description: A child was rescued by the Calgary Fire Department after being pulled from a storm pond in Legacy, but tragically succumbed to their injuries. The incident occurred on July 13, 2026, and highlights th...

item.title: Who Should Get a COVID Vaccine Now? Guidance for High-Risk Groups
item.description: The article outlines current guidelines for receiving additional COVID vaccines in Australia, focusing on high-risk groups. Older adults (75+), those with chronic conditions, and residents of aged car...

item.title: Vice President Sara Duterte's Impeachment Trial Progresses
item.description: The article provides live updates on the ongoing impeachment trial of Vice President Sara Duterte, which is entering its fifth day. Published by ABS-CBN News on July 14, 2026, the report highlights th...

item.title: Saskatchewan Experiences Record Tornado Activity Amid Climate Factors
item.description: Saskatchewan is facing an unprecedented tornado season, with 27 confirmed tornadoes touching down in 2026, surpassing the previous record set in 2012. Environment Canada reports that 12 tornadoes stru...

item.title: The Trial of Turkey's Opposition Leader: A Political and Legal Struggle
item.description: Ekrem İmamoğlu, former mayor of Istanbul and a prominent opposition figure in Turkey, faces a protracted legal battle against charges of fraud, organized crime, and other financial offenses. Detained ...

item.title: Travel agent shares client's Turkey trip challenges and resolution
item.description: A travel agent recounts a client's difficulties during a trip to Turkey, highlighting issues such as logistical hurdles, cultural misunderstandings, and unexpected delays. The agent details how they w...

item.title: James Reid Joins Baby Dolls in 'Oohh Lala Baby' Dance on It’s Showtime

item.title: US Strikes Iran Amid Hormuz Blockade and Fee Demands
item.description: The United States has launched a third night of strikes on Iranian military targets, with President Donald Trump announcing plans to impose a blockade on Iranian ports and charge a 20% fee for cargo p...

item.title: Sudbury to Compete for 2030 Memorial Cup
item.description: Sudbury, Ontario, has officially announced its intention to host the 2030 Memorial Cup, a prestigious junior ice hockey tournament in Canada. The decision comes as part of a broader effort by Northern...

item.title: Orange Group Introduces Cannon PM for Nighttime Recovery in Nigeria
item.description: Orange Group, a leading consumer healthcare and FMCG company in Nigeria, has launched Cannon PM, a premium overnight recovery solution designed to help workers combat physical discomfort and poor slee...
